role_title: Procurement Transformation Program Manager, Operations and AI

level_signals: Expected to drive large-scale transformation programs independently
Expected to coach and enable others across a global team

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of professional experience in a role requiring critical thinking, problem-solving, and cross-functional collaboration including direct experience in procurement, supply chain, finance operations, consulting, or a related operational function
Demonstrated, hands-on experience building AI-powered tools, agents, automations, or workflows that transformed real work processes (not just using AI as a chatbot — building with it)
Proficiency with AI development tools and platforms (e.g., Claude, Claude Code, Codex, custom agent frameworks, API integrations, and workflow automation tools)
Track record of coaching, teaching, or enabling others — formally or informally — with evidence that people you've helped changed how they work
Familiarity with process improvement methodologies (Lean, Six Sigma, process mapping, etc.) or structured consulting frameworks

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with change management, organizational transformation, or large-scale enablement programs
Familiarity with Procurement technology stacks (e.g., Zip, Ramp, Oracle, etc.)
Experience building and scaling internal tools, templates, or playbooks that were adopted beyond your immediate team
Background in consulting, solutions engineering, technical account management, or other client-facing technical roles
Familiarity with the payments industry and fintech landscape

scope.surface: Global Procurement organization — Strategic Sourcing, Third Party Risk, Contracting, Procurement Operations
scope.business_model: internal operations / enterprise B2B financial infrastructure
primary_metrics_inferred: Extent to which team members start tasks with an AI tool
core_responsibilities: Identify and document the highest-leverage workflow transformations within your group's day-to-day work by understanding their processes and outputs
Build and maintain a structured backlog of workflow improvement opportunities, using data and stakeholder input to sequence work effectively

Build and deploy AI-powered tools, automations, and intelligent workflows that eliminate manual effort and enhance procurement capabilities, including autonomous agents, data products, and integrations with Stripe's procurement tech stack
Work across Procurement Enablement and partner teams to uncover AI use cases, document requirements, and translate complex procurement workflows into scalable technical solutions
Coach and partner across Procurement through a progressive journey: from awareness, to first win, to regular AI integration, to full workflow transformation, to self-sufficiency
Develop operating models, SOPs, and process documentation that make improved ways of working repeatable and scalable
Teach Procurement SMEs not just to use AI tools, but to design, build, and oversee their own automated workflows over time

green_flags: Role is clearly scoped with specific sub-teams named (Strategic Sourcing, Third Party Risk, Contracting, Procurement Operations)
Explicit success metric stated: 'the number of workflows you've permanently transformed'
Encourages candidates who meet minimum requirements to apply even without preferred qualifications
Hands-on, build-oriented role rather than advisory — suggests real ownership
red_flags: No location or remote/hybrid policy stated anywhere in the JD — candidates cannot assess workplace flexibility
Broad scope ('global procurement organization') with no team size or reporting structure clarity

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 5+ years requirement and scope of autonomy expected)
scope.business_model (inferred from Stripe's product description and internal operations context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from JD language about 'measure of success')
ambiguities: Location and remote eligibility are entirely absent from the JD — no city, country, or work arrangement is mentioned. Treated as 'unclear'.

The role title says 'Program Manager' but responsibilities are heavily technical (building AI tools, writing code-adjacent automations) — could be read as Technical PM or Technical Program Manager. Classified as Technical Program Manager given program management framing with BizOps secondary archetype.
